description: I've had a great overall experience with High Q. It's ease of use, collaboration technology as well as variety of presentation tools is very valuable.
author: Ildaphonse C.
pros: It's an easy to use collaboration tools i use with developers to create presentations for clients. The cost is low as well.
cons: I don't have any cons for the product. I would sugvest more types of designs to use as well as integration with other products.

description: I love using HighQ. This tool has made my business documentation seamless. I love that I can collaborate on project managements
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is very easy to use and it is great value for money plus amazing customer support. Managing documents is easy and I can share files easily
cons: Their is noting I can say that I did not like. because I have not found anything that is a cause for concern

author: Sonny S.
pros: I most like about high q is it's excellent customer support service, and file sharing capabilities features
cons: I least liked the high learning curve of HighQ and it's challenging user interface to learn how to use
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: HighQ is a robust investment that is highly valued by medium to large law firms and corporate legal departments that require top-tier security and a flexible toolset to manage the full lifecycle of legal projects and client engagement. However, potential users must be prepared for a significant investment in both cost and training to fully unlock its advanced capabilities.
author: Christa B.
pros: It Offers tools for visualizing data (like task statuses, activity trends, and key metrics) through customizable dashboards, allowing teams to monitor performance and gain insights.
cons: As a comprehensive, enterprise-level platform from Thomson Reuters, HighQ is generally not cheap. Pricing is often high and dependent on the number of users and storage requirements.

review_title: Legal help on a new level
description: Overall, I like being able to configure dashboards, track project progress, and visualize key data to help my team stay informed and make better decisions.
author: Ryan P.
pros: HighQ lets you securely share, store, and collaborate on documents and legal materials in one centralized platform.
cons: The platform is expensive, especially for smaller firms or teams, and that value depends heavily on how deeply you use its feature set.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The ability to build automated workflows is terrific. The ability to create specific views is great. The amount of information one can store is pretty good. It's useful to have one Thomson Reuters representative.
author: fatima R.
pros: In my 2+ years of using HighQ Collaborate on a daily basis, the simplicity of the UI along with the platform's robustness has been a major plus point. Our IT team was able to set it up in a very bespoke manner to suit our project parameters and we haven't really needed to tweak or change things since. The export and import functionality has been a literal lifesaver as its allowed us to really optimise our workflows to efficiently deal with varying tasks & instructions, and to carry data from Collaborate to our other tools. We were also able to use inbuilt reporting system to develop a quick MI tool for progress tracking at a glance. In the few instances when there have been outages, we found the Customer Support to be top notch & they provided us realistic ETA's instead of telling us what they thought we wanted to hear.
cons: There are no downsides as such, but there has been a recurring glitch, where forms submitted for a task, will reappear at times in the middle of my queue if I've got filters on. This can sometimes get really disorienting as I see work I've already completed re-appear in my queue.

description: My overall experience with HighQ was a pleasant one. I use it on a daily basis and my job has become much more organized and efficient because of it.
author: Meghan A.
pros: What I liked the most about the HighQ app was it helped me manage documents in a safe place everyday.
cons: The thing I liked the least about HighQ is it took a minute to understand the whole layout but once I did it worked out very well.

description: Thisa powerful, indispensable platform for legal professionals seeking advanced collaboration, security, and efficiency, offering a strong return on investment once fully implemented and customized, though firms should be prepared for potential costs and training investment.
author: Cory R.
pros: Enables branded, secure client portals for easy, controlled sharing and engagement. helpful and responsive implementation and ongoing support.
cons: the interface dull or clients express disappointment; administrative tasks, like sorting choice lists, can be manual and time-consuming.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Game-Changer for Secure Document and Project Management
description: HighQ has truly transformed how I handle projects and client interactions. The peace of mind from its top-tier security for sensitive documents is invaluable. Setting up secure, branded client portals is seamless, significantly enhancing our professional image and client collaboration. Internally, it centralizes all project information – documents, tasks, and communications – eliminating chaos. The workflow automation saves us significant time. While there was a slight learning curve, the benefits of improved efficiency, security, and professional client engagement have made HighQ an indispensable part of my daily workflow.
author: Devonte E.
pros: I like most is that I can securely share information and collaborate. I can also coordinate task and review deadlines
cons: The learning curve for such a extensive feature set can be very steep and overwhelming. sometimes it can be challenging to locate specific settings
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: HighQ, Gets the job done, but in a dull way.
description: My company uses it for legal compliance in document sharing with our clients.
author: Wes P.
pros: It has an intuitive simple to use interface. It easily meets all requirements for document sharing with our clients.
cons: Some clients have expressed disappointment with the user interface offered them.

description: Sharing documents in a secure way is easy with HighQ. I am confident that the data put onto the system is secure, and I am also confident that only the users granted access can see the documents I want them to see.
author: Anne T.
pros: Uploading and structuring documents was very easy, which is key when sharing important legal documents with a client.
cons: Sometimes the software doesn't work, as in access to the web browser is down. This is less than ideal if you need to share large documents with clients urgently.

review_title: Worth it for small law departments
description: Very positive experience! We are doing work of a senior paralegal/junior attorney through automation and administration by a legal assistant. Highly efficient and once heavy lifting on automation was complete, easy to implement with users as very user friendly.
author: Rachael P.
pros: I know that the contracts being signed are done properly without attorney oversight. I can spend my time doing actual drafting work vs. more administrative customization work. Made contracting more self-service to the business and will continue to implement this more over time. High version control and easy to update standard language and know the next contract to be signed will include it without version control issues and training the business.
cons: Wish there were more contract management features for organizing and managing existing executed agreements. Had to complete work around through Datasheets.

review_title: Contract Express - Best Document Automation Out There
description: We're capable of generating estate planning document packages that can contain up to 15 documents and total more than 200 pages, and they're all driven by a single questionnaire within Contract Express. The days of manually assembling complex documents are over.
author: Robert S.
pros: Contract Express makes it very easy to automate documents. You can program small, simple documents in a very short amount of time. It's so intuitive that your average power user could learn how to automate a document in 20 minutes, so not every project requires someone with programming experience. However, the software is also capable of scaling up to handle complex, multi-document packages with complex branching logic as well.
cons: This is a minor quibble, but the documentation at the time (back in 2016) was somewhat lacking. While their training and support teams were amazing and responsive, we sometimes just wanted to dive in and read a manual and learn for ourselves, but we had a tough time finding the answers we needed without getting a person on the phone.

author: Jason S.
pros: This software is a must have for any law firm or in-house legal department. It is well designed, functions without material flaw, and continues to be improved. It materially increases the speed and accuracy of contract deployment and that is just the begining.
cons: The written knowledge base/manual could and should be improved.

description: I have only been using ContractExpress for about 2 months, but it has been extremely beneficial in helping our customers streamline creating forms for their clients. Looking forward to many more months!
author: Amelia F.
pros: The customer service has always been second to none. I've sent over a handful of questions, and I always get a response back the same day!
cons: Using this software was a steep learning curve for me, but with only a couple of hours of training, I caught on very quickly.
author_profession: Legal Editor and Document Automation Specialist
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Sophie W.
pros: integrated platform, easy to use, friendly.
cons: nothing.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Georg R.
pros: Ease of use. Compatibility and adaptability.
cons: Cost is higher than expected. Problems on start-up.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Pretty good, i'm a newer user
author: Marissa C.
pros: Easy accessibility, nice layout, user friendly
cons: Searches can come up with irrelevant results
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: bob N.
pros: Breadth of features are good. Interesting possibilities for expansion of features into new item.
cons: Perceived effort in tailoring systems and due dates. Unsure on how its features overlap or not with existing systems.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Kazumasa W.
pros: I think the most useful aspect is design.
cons: I think the least useful aspect of this product is expertise
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It takes a while to learn to use
author: Chris L.
pros: Has some advantages, but overall it has a learning curve.
cons: It can be pricy for what you get, but you need to use all features for the price.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Secure and powerful, but not the easiest to use
author: Elon E.
pros: What I liked most about HighQ is its secure and well-structured collaboration environment - it makes document sharing, workflow management, and client interaction very efficient. It’s especially strong for managing sensitive data, with good automation and customization options, even if it takes a bit of time to fully master.
cons: What I liked least about HighQ is that it can feel a bit complex and not very intuitive at first, so there’s a real learning curve. The interface isn’t always the most modern, and setup or customization can take time. Pricing is also relatively high compared to other collaboration tools.

description: Overall, my experience with HighQ was positive. It was helpful for keeping documents organized, sharing files, and managing work in a more central place. The biggest challenge was getting comfortable with the platform in the beginning, but after that it became much easier to use.
author: Chanda Q.
pros: What I liked most about HighQ was how organized it kept everything. It made document management, file sharing, and collaboration easier because everything was in one place instead of spread across different tools. Once I got used to it, it helped make work feel more structured and easier to track.
cons: What I liked least was the learning curve. At first, some parts of the system were not very intuitive, so it took a little time to figure out where everything was and how to use certain features efficiently. It can feel a little overwhelming when you first start using it.

review_title: Reliable Platform for Document Management and Team Collaboration
description: Overall I've had a good experience with HighQ for managing documents and working with others it's a good deal and makes sharing files easy however it can take some time to learn and support can be slow at times.
author: Muhammad K.
pros: The best things about HighQ for me were how well it handled documents and how well it let teams work together it is pretty easy to use lets you share files quickly and is a good deal for professional work needs,
cons: The thing I liked least about HighQ was that it can take a while to get used to especially for new users sometimes customer service may take a while and in some ways the whole experience may not be as intuitive as with simpler tools.

review_title: Secure and powerful Collaboration for Legal Teams
description: HighQ makes it easy to manage documents securely and work with other on a team but the price are high and some advanced feature take some time to learn. Support and billing are also reliable.
author: Sandra J.
pros: I liked HighQ's strong documents management and secure file sharing. It was easy for legal teams to use, helped teams work together well, and had advanced controls. The price was good for complicated project customer support was quick to respond and billing was clear but a little high.
cons: HighQ can b expensive for small teams and its advanced feature take some time to learn which makes onboarding a little hard.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
